symptoms.symptom_name: Deep painful coalescing oral ulcers with fibrinous pseudomembrane

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep coalescing fibrinous ulceration is characteristic of WHO Grade 3-4 mucositis.
Candidiasis produces superficial wipeable plaques rather than deep ulcers.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe odynophagia with inability to swallow any fluids for ~14 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Odynophagia severe enough to stop all intake reflects extensive mucosal ulceration and drives the dehydration complication.
Total cessation of oral intake is the direct cause of early volume depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent chemotherapy ~10 days prior (neutrophil nadir window)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucositis peaks at 7-14 days post-chemotherapy; the timeline is definitional.
The nadir places the patient at high risk of neutropenia, making any fever a sepsis emergency.
Below the 38.0 C threshold now, but in a chemo patient it is a live tripwire — a small rise makes this an emergency; patient-obtainable and admissible in telehealth but unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick ropy saliva, xerostomia and reduced urine output

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Concentrated ropy saliva and reduced output are early clinical markers of volume depletion.
Non-specific but, combined with the nadir and borderline temperature, raises concern for an evolving infective process.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis / no heavy mouth bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of bleeding makes active thrombocytopenic haemorrhage less likely now, but it remains a safety-net trigger.
Compensatory tachycardia is an objective marker of early volume depletion — a sealed clinician measurement the AI cannot obtain remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Chemotherapy-induced oral mucositis with local inflammation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reactive nodes to oral inflammation/infection; requires palpation, sealed as a clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood examination / absolute neutrophil count

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood examination with differential (absolute neutrophil count)
investigation_details.expected_result: Not available; needed to exclude neutropenia at the chemotherapy nadir
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Neutropenia and evolving neutropenic sepsis cannot be excluded — the reason urgent in-person assessment is mandatory.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ANC is the pivotal test; it cannot be obtained via telehealth, which is exactly why the AI must route to in-person care.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Post-chemotherapy severe mucositis with failed oral intake
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Chemotherapy-induced oral mucositis with early dehydration
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The triad of deep oral ulceration, total odynophagia and the 7-14 day post-chemo timeline is the defining pattern and the trigger for urgent supportive care.
Recent chemo + borderline temperature + fatigue is the constellation that must convert to an emergency response the moment temperature reaches 38.0 C.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination with differential (absolute neutrophil count)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude neutropenia at the chemotherapy nadir — the pivotal reason in-person assessment is required; cannot be done via telehealth.
Assess dehydration severity and screen for acute kidney injury from reduced intake.
Mandatory before empirical antibiotics if temperature reaches the febrile-neutropenia threshold.
Only if secondary infection is suspected; not first-line and should not delay analgesia, hydration or neutropenia screening.
